title: Part 4 – Discussion (C1 Cambridge)
description: Evaluate the depth and sophistication of your opinions in the final discussion with the examiner.
prompt: Acts as an official examiner for the Cambridge C1 Advanced (CAE). Evaluate my intervention in Part 4 (Discussion) according to the official Cambridge criteria: Grammar and Vocabulary, Discourse Management, Pronunciation and Interactive Communication. Rate each criterion from 1 to 5. Identify the fillers I have used and suggest alternatives C1. Next, give me 5 C1 level expressions that fit what I said. Here is my intervention: [PASTE YOUR TEXT]

criteria.name: Grammar and Vocabulary
criteria.description: Use of abstract vocabulary and complex structures to express and defend reasoned opinions.

criteria.name: Discourse Management
criteria.description: Ability to develop and sustain extensive and well-structured arguments before the examiner.

criteria.name: Pronunciation
criteria.description: Fluency and clarity when holding a more abstract and longer conversation.

criteria.name: Interactive Communication
criteria.description: Ability to respond to the examiner's questions in a relevant way, expanding and justifying ideas.

tips: Expand your answers with arguments and examples: never answer with a simple sentence.
Express nuances in your opinion: 'To a considerable extent...', 'While this is generally true, there are exceptions...'
Use abstract vocabulary: 'socioeconomic implications', 'cultural ramifications', 'a paradigm shift', 'unprecedented challenges'
Anticipa contraargumentos: 'One might argue that..., however...', 'Critics of this view would suggest...'
Relaciona con el tema general: 'This connects to the broader issue of...', 'In the wider context of...'

speakingFeatures.focus: Extended discussion of abstract topics related to the collaborative task
speakingFeatures.examinerRole: Asks questions to both candidates to elicit extended, substantiated opinions
speakingFeatures.commonMistake: Giving short, undeveloped answers instead of extended, substantiated responses
